title: The Ink‑Stained Lamp of Whitby Hall
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitmore, renowned antiquarian, was found dead in the modest library with a lamp, a book, a mug and a chair. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Pembroke
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Clara Haversham helped me move the chair clear of the doorway. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Victor Lyle and Eleanor Whitmore were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Clara Haversham and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Clara Haversham
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, while Gareth Pembroke moved the chair clear of the doorway, I returned the book squarely to its place. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Pembroke and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Victor Lyle
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the lamp and did not go near the book.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

clues.title: The Dented Lamp
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the lamp. Eleanor Whitmore has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Movement of Chair
clues.detail: A fresh floor scrape made by the chair is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Pembroke and Clara Haversham both state that they moved the chair clear of the doorway at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Lamp
clues.detail: Teal-and-cream tweed fibres are caught inside a fresh split beneath the base of the lamp. Victor Lyle's teal tweed jacket cuff has a newly frayed cuff edge, exposing the same unusual teal-and-cream check.

clues.title: Position of Book
clues.detail: A clean rectangular outline left when the book was moved is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack. Clara Haversham states that they returned the book squarely to its place at 8:35 pm immediately before leaving the room.

clues.title: Crack in Mug
clues.detail: A crack runs across the mug. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Victor Lyle killed Eleanor Whitmore because the imminent exposure of Victor's embezzlement of the society's funds.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitmore was killed by a heavy blow from the lamp.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Victor Lyle claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The physical trace on the chair corroborates the action described by Gareth Pembroke, while the trace on the book corroborates the action described by Clara Haversham. They left together at 8:35 pm, and both traces are crossed by evidence from the later attack. Meanwhile, teal-and-cream tweed fibres on the lamp directly contradict Victor Lyle's denial and place them at the murder object during the attack window.
